message:
fix(ui): use display_width for dashboard label measurement and truncation

The analytics dashboard's agent/workspace bar chart labels were using
.len() (byte count) and .chars().take(n) (character count) for width
calculation and truncation. Both are incorrect for Unicode text — CJK
characters are 2 columns wide, and multi-byte characters cause
misalignment when used with column-based terminal layouts.

Changes:
- Label width measurement: .len().min(14) → display_width(name).min(14)
  so the column budget correctly accounts for wide characters.
- Label truncation: .chars().take(n) → shorten_label(name, n) which
  uses display_width internally and properly handles grapheme clusters.
- Label padding: format!("{:<width$}") → explicit " ".repeat(pad_w)
  since std::fmt's width specifier counts characters, not terminal
  columns, producing misaligned output with CJK or emoji text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
